response.justification: Strong, seasoned polyglot engineer with genuine high-throughput/distributed-systems exposure (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async services) and directly relevant current work on MCP code generators and agentic tooling at re:cinq. Software-craftsmanship depth (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based) and Staff-level titles fit the seniority bar. However, the must-have 'Strong Python' is absent from the skill set and history (stack is TypeScript/Node, DLang, Java, C#), and there's little evidence of formal technical-strategy/stakeholder leadership at scale or operational on-call ownership. Worth an interview to probe Python depth and strategy scope.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed-systems experience (Sociomantic micro-services)
Direct agentic/AI-native exposure: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks
Deep craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, strong TypeScript across full stack
concerns: No Python evidence despite it being a must-have core skill
Limited demonstrated technical-strategy and cross-functional stakeholder leadership at scale
No clear track record of operational ownership / on-call incident response
